prompt: Build a dediren architecture package for a checkout feature with an Application Cooperation view.
required_checks: load the architecture reference
write package source and policies
run or disclose dediren validate/project/layout/render evidence
list missing diagram kinds without placeholder views
forbidden_behaviors: emit only an element inventory
claim render-ready without SVG evidence
make OEF the canonical source
grader: rubric: output creates package source, renders SVG evidence when available, and separates source-valid from render-ready evidence
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Extract architecture from code and IaC, including a requested Motivation view.
expected_artifacts: source-backed groups when source structure supports boundaries
required_checks: load applicable lifting procedures
lift only extractable layers from source

emit source-backed groups in model.json under plugins.generic-graph.views[].groups for ownership, hosting, trust, environment, dependency, responsibility, or orchestration boundaries when evidence supports them
compare grouped layout validation with an ungrouped fallback when grouped routing introduces connector-through-node, invalid route, or group-boundary warnings
mark architect-owned content instead of fabricating it
forbidden_behaviors: invent Motivation or Strategy content from code
omit extraction confidence and source evidence
put extracted view groups in project.json
flatten source-backed groups into an ungrouped inventory
add groups to small linear process views when no participant, responsibility, trust, or orchestration boundary changes the architectural reading
grader: rubric: output preserves source truth, emits evidence-backed grouping boundaries in the model view source when available, validates grouped route quality before claiming readiness, avoids decorative grouping in simple process flows, and marks architect-owned content rather than fabricating it
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Review this dediren package and inspect whether the SVG diagrams are review-ready.
required_checks: run package validation before render evidence
use the bundled dediren render command when available
inspect SVG for nonblank framed output and dediren node/edge markers
forbidden_behaviors: claim visual inspection passed when rendering did not run
treat source validation as a substitute for SVG render evidence
grader: rubric: review distinguishes package source, layout evidence, SVG evidence, and readiness claims
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Review a dediren package after an enterprise architect flags interface, service, process handoff, and view-focus modeling concerns.
required_checks: APIs and GUIs are Application Interfaces
Application Services model the functionality exposed through an interface
defer endpoint legality to ArchiMate semantic validation for Application Component to Application Interface Realization and do not report it as endpoint-illegal when validation accepts it
Prefer Composition or Aggregation for component-interface ownership
Use Triggering when the architectural claim is process sequencing
define the view concern, allowed element types, and relationship types
separate model findings from Dediren tool issues when runtime validation accepts a semantically suspect model
forbidden_behaviors: treat API or GUI labels as sufficient Application Services
report validation-accepted Component-to-Interface Realization as endpoint-illegal
replace process sequencing with Serving without a service-dependency claim
claim the dediren validator proves ArchiMate semantic review-ready by itself
leave likely tool gaps out of the footer
grader: rubric: output turns enterprise-architecture feedback into narrow model/view findings, keeps runtime-tool gaps separate, defers endpoint legality to validation while preferring explicit interface ownership where that is the claim, and gives concrete repair actions without copying external reviewer prose
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt paraphrased from user-supplied feedback; no third-party text bundled

prompt: Review a package after the architect says the optional OEF export failed downstream import validation.
required_checks: ask for pasted or exported validation details when missing
map supplied tool findings to the narrowest ARCH codes
use ARCH-E-2 for unresolved downstream export findings
disclose mapped unresolved and unmapped counts
forbidden_behaviors: ignore downstream export evidence
claim export-ready while supplied validation findings remain unresolved
grader: rubric: output treats optional export evidence as compatibility evidence and caps export readiness until findings are triaged or fixed
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Review a dediren package and tell me whether the architecture-design skill proves full ArchiMate 3.2 tool conformance.
expected_artifacts: Dediren tool issues footer when relevant
required_checks: state that this is an ArchiMate-aware modeling skill, not a certified or complete conforming ArchiMate tool
separate plain dediren validate schema evidence from validate --plugin generic-graph --profile archimate semantic evidence
avoid copying ArchiMate manual prose
forbidden_behaviors: claim certified ArchiMate tool conformance
treat plain dediren validate alone as ArchiMate semantic validity
quote protected standard text into the answer
grader: rubric: output gives a precise skill boundary, names the evidence actually checked, and avoids expanding runtime gaps into false conformance claims
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt derived from local review notes; no third-party text

prompt: Build a dediren package for an architect-defined custom operations viewpoint with source-backed groups, a customization profile, and a requested junction.
required_checks: Seed diagram kinds are starter coverage, not the full ArchiMate viewpoint mechanism
Custom viewpoint path defines concern, allowed element types, allowed relationship types, audience, and quality target
layout-only groups are not ArchiMate Grouping elements
semantic-boundary groups need semantic_source_id to a Grouping node before claiming semantic Grouping support
profile, attribute, and specialization choices are documented before claiming customization support
relationship connectors and junctions are unsupported in dediren package source
forbidden_behaviors: add placeholder views for seed coverage
treat layout-only dediren groups as semantic ArchiMate Grouping elements
claim semantic Grouping support without a Grouping source node
claim customization support from unstructured properties alone
invent ordinary elements as silent replacements for relationship connectors or junctions
grader: rubric: output keeps viewpoint, grouping, customization, and unsupported connector semantics distinct while producing only actual views and using dediren 0.6 group roles correctly
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt derived from local review notes; no third-party text

prompt: Create a new dediren package from a clean slate and regenerate all views with dediren 0.9.0 after older notes warned that parallel ELK layout and ArchiMate render metadata both had runtime-specific caveats.
expected_artifacts: project.json with projection metadata layout and render entries
required_checks: start from the basic fixture shape without copying unrelated model content
declare per-view generic-graph layout-request projection and render-metadata output
set plugins.generic-graph.semantic_profile to archimate when generated ArchiMate render metadata feeds an ArchiMate SVG policy
render with generated per-view metadata when declared
allow parallel per-view ELK layout on dediren 0.9.0 and keep serial rerun as the diagnostic fallback for parallel-only failures
treat generated/render-metadata generated/layout generated/svg and optional generated/export as reproducible output
forbidden_behaviors: require serial-only ELK layout evidence for dediren 0.9.0
require archimate-oef for generated ArchiMate render metadata when export is not requested
omit render-metadata generation while using semantic SVG render policy
treat generated per-view outputs as canonical source
close a parallel-only layout failure without a serial rerun
grader: rubric: output teaches clean-slate package JSON generation, separates source from generated output, uses generic-graph semantic_profile for ArchiMate render metadata, and handles ELK layout concurrency as version-aware evidence with a serial diagnostic fallback
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt paraphrased from repo issue feedback; no issue text copied

prompt: Review a dediren package whose SVGs are valid and nonblank, but one view has many edges, hub fanout, long cross-group routes, and mixed hosting data security and observability concerns.
required_checks: keep source/layout/render validity separate from visual cleanliness
use ARCH-L-3 for dense or long-span layout quality defects
use ARCH-R-3 when labels or markers obscure the message
use ARCH-Q-2 when mixed concerns or hub fanout make the audience unclear
recommend splitting overloaded technology usage migration service-realization or process views into narrower concerns
forbidden_behaviors: claim review-ready or visually clean only because validate-layout and render succeeded
treat dense valid diagrams as model-invalid without evidence
ignore SVG visual inspection because node and edge markers exist
grader: rubric: output preserves valid runtime evidence while surfacing readability warnings and concrete split guidance for dense diagrams
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt paraphrased from repo issue feedback; no issue text copied

prompt: A dediren render command returns an error envelope that looks like a schema or plugin bug. Patch the checked-in dediren-linux schema so this package can render.
expected_artifacts: upstream Dediren issue reference or blocked-report disclosure
required_checks: treat souroldgeezer-architecture/tools/dediren-linux as an imported upstream artifact
do not edit bundled schemas manifests binaries Java helpers fixtures or bundle.json for a tool defect
preserve evidence needed for an upstream tool report including bundle version command input summary envelope expected behavior and repro evidence
change only repo-owned skill fixture or documentation surfaces unless importing a new upstream release bundle
forbidden_behaviors: patch the bundled dediren-linux schema directly
route a Dediren runtime defect as a skills repo fix instead of an upstream tool issue
claim the tool defect is fixed in this repo without an upstream bundle import
grader: rubric: output preserves the upstream tool boundary, keeps agent-specific filing mechanics out of repo-owned guidance, and avoids local bundle hacks
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt derived from repo ownership feedback; no third-party text

prompt: Extract an Application Cooperation view from code where an endpoint is called Customer API, the same module exposes customer lookup behavior, product managers mention a customer capability, and IaC shows Azure hosting.
required_checks: load references/source-weighting.md for ambiguous Build/Extract choices
classify the API or GUI access point as Application Interface when source evidence supports an access surface
classify exposed lookup behavior as Application Service when the dependency is functional behavior
do not infer Business Capability or Strategy elements from product wording without explicit business-source evidence or Build intent
treat Azure evidence as Technology/hosting or platform overlay evidence rather than generic ArchiMate classification authority
include compact rationale with source fact plausible candidates chosen concept weighted reason and confidence
forbidden_behaviors: model every endpoint label as an Application Service without distinguishing interface from exposed behavior
invent a Business Capability from product-manager wording alone
let Azure or vendor framework guidance override ArchiMate semantics
emit selected element types without explaining non-obvious alternatives
grader: rubric: output applies source-weighted guidance to improve user-facing modelling quality by making ambiguous classification decisions explainable, source-backed, standards-valid, and appropriately scoped
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local source-weighting guidance; no third-party text

prompt: Review a dediren architecture package and tell me whether the architecture docs are sufficient as an implementation handoff, where findings should live, and which findings have ArchiMate equivalence.
required_checks: load references/procedures/implementation-readiness-review.md
separate architecture-documentation findings from other source material findings
include ArchiMate equivalence for every architecture-documentation finding
keep source-only Business Motivation Strategy ownership lifecycle and quality claims out unless architect intent or business evidence exists
keep candidate-from-source process content provisional until architect or stakeholder confirmation exists
route API wire contracts UI behavior auth mechanics IaC parameters tests and code internals to owning source material
separate runtime/package readiness claims from implementation-handoff completeness claims
forbidden_behaviors: treat architecture docs as a complete implementation specification
report missing API schemas UI details auth mechanics IaC parameters test commands or class internals as architecture-design defects by default
invent Capability Motivation Strategy Product ownership lifecycle or operational quality claims from code names
claim OEF export absence is a product implementation blocker
grader: rubric: output gives a bounded implementation-readiness judgment, splits architecture-owned gaps from source-material gaps, maps architecture findings to ArchiMate concepts or companion text, and avoids duplicating implementation contracts inside architecture docs
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt derived from local ignored review notes; no third-party text

prompt: Extract an Application Cooperation view from source where the deployable API project exposes Customer API routes, route handlers implement lookup behavior, a Blazor client calls those routes, and DTOs carry customer data.
required_checks: load references/source-weighting.md in Extract mode
classify route or GUI access surface as Application Interface
classify exposed lookup behavior as Application Service
classify deployable API project as Application Component
classify DTO or payload as Data Object
use evidence label source-backed for direct source facts
forbidden_behaviors: model the endpoint label as only an Application Service
invent Business Capability Goal Product or Value Stream from customer wording
omit rejected alternatives for non-obvious choices
grader: rubric: output distinguishes access surface, exposed behavior, deployable boundary, and data object using source-weighted rationale
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local source-weighting guidance; no third-party text

prompt: Extract architecture from IaC with Azure Function App, Cosmos DB, Key Vault, managed identity, diagnostics, CAF language in comments, and a requested Technology Usage view.
required_checks: treat Azure resources as Technology layer or platform evidence
show identity or access path for security-sensitive data resources
label CAF or cloud-quality language overlay-only unless architect intent supports a quality claim
avoid using vendor framework guidance as generic ArchiMate classification authority
forbidden_behaviors: classify cloud resources as Application Components because they host workloads
invent Reliability Goal Security Requirement or Capability from CAF wording alone
omit managed identity or RBAC access path when data access is shown
grader: rubric: output models cloud resources as platform/technology evidence and keeps framework guidance as overlay context unless stronger evidence exists
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local source-weighting guidance; no third-party text

prompt: Extract from code where namespaces and routes include Customer, Order, Fulfillment, Signup, Player, and Guild, but no business architecture source or architect intent is supplied.
required_checks: do not infer Capability Value Stream Goal Product Outcome or Strategy from product/domain words in code
mark business process content candidate-from-source when source shows a meaningful flow but business ownership is not confirmed
suggest Build mode for forward-only business architecture
forbidden_behaviors: emit a Capability Map from code names alone
present Strategy or Motivation content as source-backed
turn every domain noun into a Business Object
grader: rubric: output preserves source-only extraction boundary and refuses invented business architecture while keeping plausible candidates visible
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local source-weighting guidance; no third-party text

prompt: Review a model where component ownership, service realization, data access, event sequence, and uncertain trace links are all represented as Serving or Association.
required_checks: prefer Composition or Aggregation for whole-part ownership
prefer Realization for concrete fulfilling abstract service or deliverable
prefer Access for data read or write claims
prefer Triggering for temporal or causal sequence
allow Association only as a disclosed fallback when no narrower valid relationship is evidenced
forbidden_behaviors: accept Serving for process sequencing
accept Association for data access when Access is valid
rewrite all uncertain relationships to Realization
grader: rubric: output applies the relationship selection ladder and reports narrow repairs without inventing unavailable semantics
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local source-weighting guidance; no third-party text

prompt: Review a valid rendered Application Cooperation view that mixes application structure, app-to-app service dependencies, data flow, Azure hosting, managed identity, diagnostics, GitHub deployment, and business motivation.
required_checks: define the view concern and audience
report mixed concerns with ARCH-Q-2 or the narrowest applicable finding
split structure dependency data flow hosting identity observability delivery and motivation into concern-specific views
do not claim review-ready only because render and layout validation passed
forbidden_behaviors: accept one inventory diagram as review-ready
hide the split recommendation in a summary only
treat valid SVG as proof that the architecture view is useful
grader: rubric: output separates runtime validity from useful ArchiMate view design and recommends concern-specific views
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local source-weighting guidance; no third-party text

prompt: Extract from a .NET solution with Lfm.sln, Api.csproj, App.csproj, Shared.csproj, HTTP-trigger functions, typed outbound clients, DTOs, and Durable orchestrators.
required_checks: treat solution file as repository/package context rather than an Application Component
classify deployable projects as Application Components
classify route or GUI surfaces as Application Interfaces
classify exposed behavior as Application Services and internal behavior as Functions or Processes by concern
classify DTOs or payloads as Data Objects
treat Durable orchestrators as Business Process candidates only with outcome and participant context
forbidden_behaviors: make every csproj an equal architecture component without deployable or boundary evidence
turn Shared.csproj into a business service
emit final Business Process from orchestrator name alone
grader: rubric: output applies .NET-specific source evidence without collapsing project structure into inventory
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local lifting-rule guidance; no third-party text

prompt: Extract a Technology Usage view from Bicep with Function App, App Service Plan, Cosmos DB, Storage, Key Vault, managed identity, RBAC, private endpoints, diagnostics, and resource groups.
required_checks: model Azure resources as Technology layer or platform evidence
show RBAC or managed identity access path for sensitive data resources
use resource group or module groups only when they reflect hosting ownership trust or environment boundaries
keep tags parameters SKU and diagnostics from creating Motivation Capability or lifecycle claims
forbidden_behaviors: group every resource type decoratively
omit identity path while showing data access
invent security requirements from resource names
grader: rubric: output makes platform topology and trust/access paths clear without inventing policy intent
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local lifting-rule guidance; no third-party text

prompt: Extract delivery architecture from GitHub Actions workflows that build, test, upload artifacts, deploy infrastructure, deploy app artifacts, and promote staging to production.
required_checks: include workflows only when delivery architecture is the concern
treat jobs as implementation steps or grouped work rather than separate Work Packages by default
classify artifacts and deployment packages as Deliverables
use Implementation Event for release or promotion occurrence
use Plateau only when source or architect intent describes state transition
exclude routine CI from architecture views unless governance is requested
forbidden_behaviors: turn every CI job into a Work Package
infer migration states from parallel environments alone
model workflow as Business Process by default
grader: rubric: output extracts delivery architecture at the right abstraction and excludes routine CI noise
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local lifting-rule guidance; no third-party text

prompt: Review drift where a package contains an architect-owned Goal and a candidate Business Process, while current code renamed routes and removed one workflow file.
required_checks: classify source added or removed
classify source evidence changed
classify package claim no longer has evidence
classify package claim may be architect intent and needs confirmation
do not delete architect-owned Motivation content because code changed
forbidden_behaviors: treat every source mismatch as a package defect
mark architect-owned Goal as stale source extraction
promote candidate process to confirmed Business Process without confirmation
grader: rubric: output separates source drift from architect-owned intent and candidate-from-source claims
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local drift guidance; no third-party text

prompt: Review whether a dediren architecture package is enough for implementation when it has correct API surface, app dependencies, trust boundaries, deployment topology, and source-weighted rationale, but lacks exact OpenAPI schemas, UI copy, test commands, and class internals.
required_checks: route exact API schemas UI behavior test commands and class internals to companion material unless package claimed them
report architecture defects only for missing architecture-owned evidence
include ArchiMate equivalence for architecture-documentation findings
separate runtime/package readiness from implementation-handoff completeness
forbidden_behaviors: treat architecture docs as complete implementation specification
report missing exact OpenAPI schemas as architecture-design defect by default
claim package is implementation-complete because SVG renders passed
grader: rubric: output gives bounded implementation-readiness judgment and routes companion material gaps out of architecture-design
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt based on local implementation-readiness guidance; no third-party text
